SEARCH
Package edu.bu.pas.risk.territory

Record Class TerritoryCard

java.lang.Object
java.lang.Record
edu.bu.pas.risk.territory.TerritoryCard
Record Components:
territory - The territory visible on this card (if your agent owns this territory during redemption, that territory receives +2 armies). Will be null for wild cards.
armyValue - The value of the army present on the card (has no actual effect, just effects how cards are redeemed).
isWild - true if this is a wild card.
All Implemented Interfaces:
Cloneable
public record TerritoryCard(Territory territory, int armyValue, boolean isWild) extends Record implements Cloneable
Whenever an agent conquers a territory, and they have not already been rewarded a card this turn, then they receive a territory card which can be redeemed for free armies.

In a standard game of risk, there are 44 cards. We get those 44 cards by having 2 wild cards, and 1 card for each of the 42 territories.

At the start of an agent's turn, they may choose to redeem 3 cards using RedeemCardsAction. See isValidGrouping(List) for the rules on how to redeem cards. See getRedemptionAmount(int) to see how many armies are redeemed.

See Also:
  • RedeemCardsAction

  • Constructor Details

    • TerritoryCard

      public TerritoryCard(Territory territory, int armyValue, boolean isWild)
      Creates an instance of a TerritoryCard record class.
      Parameters:
      territory - the value for the territory record component
      armyValue - the value for the armyValue record component
      isWild - the value for the isWild record component

  • Method Details

    • clone

      public TerritoryCard clone()
      Returns a deep copy of this territory card.
      Overrides:
      clone in class Object
      Returns:
      A cloned copy of this card.

    • getRedemptionAmount

      public static int getRedemptionAmount(int redemptionCount)
      Calculates the number of armies you will receive if you redeem a set of 3 territory cards.

      In risk, the number of armies you get from redeeming a territory card increases over time, so 1 person can get a large number of armies and end the game to prevent it from lasting forever.

      Parameters:
      redemptionCount - The num of this redemption... e.g. numPreviousRedemptions + 1
      Returns:
      The number of armies you receive.

    • generateCards

      public static List<TerritoryCard> generateCards(Board board)

      In risk, each territory gets 1 card, and then there are 2 wild cards.

      We autogenerate cards to try and have a uniform distribution of the 3 different army types.

      Parameters:
      board - The board to pull the territories from.
      Returns:
      The generated list of cards.

    • hasValidTrade

      public static boolean hasValidTrade(List<TerritoryCard> cards)
      Returns true iff any combination of 3 cards from the given list forms a valid grouping that can be traded in for armies.
      Parameters:
      cards - The agent's full card inventory.
      Returns:
      true if at least one valid trade exists.
      See Also:
      • isValidGrouping(List)

    • isValidGrouping

      public static boolean isValidGrouping(List<TerritoryCard> cards)
      Returns true iff one of the following conditions are met:
      • 3 cards of the same army value
      • 1 of each of the 3 army values
      • any 2 plus a wild card

      Note that you can only redeem 3 cards at a time, and you may only redeem 1 wild card in a group at a time.

      Parameters:
      cards - The list of 3 cards to check.
      Returns:
      true if the 3 cards can be traded in for armies.

    • getValidGroupings

      public static List<List<TerritoryCard>> getValidGroupings(List<TerritoryCard> cards)
      Returns an immutable list of all valid combinations of cards you can turn in.
      Parameters:
      cards - The cards owned by the player.
      Returns:
      All legal combinations of trade-ins.
      See Also:
      • RedeemCardsAction(int, List)

    • toString

      public final String toString()
      Returns a string representation of this record class. The representation contains the name of the class, followed by the name and value of each of the record components.
      Specified by:
      toString in class Record
      Returns:
      a string representation of this object

    • hashCode

      public final int hashCode()
      Returns a hash code value for this object. The value is derived from the hash code of each of the record components.
      Specified by:
      hashCode in class Record
      Returns:
      a hash code value for this object

    • equals

      public final boolean equals(Object o)
      Indicates whether some other object is "equal to" this one. The objects are equal if the other object is of the same class and if all the record components are equal. Reference components are compared with Objects::equals(Object,Object); primitive components are compared with '=='.
      Specified by:
      equals in class Record
      Parameters:
      o - the object with which to compare
      Returns:
      true if this object is the same as the o argument; false otherwise.
